steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: List the known angles. Triangle $DEF$: $\angle D = 40^\circ$, $\angle E = 95^\circ$. Triangle $RST$: $\angle R = 40^\circ$, $\angle S = 95^\circ$. We already have two pairs of equal corresponding angles: $\angle D = \angle R$ and $\angle E = \angle S$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: The AA (Angle-Angle) Similarity Theorem states: if two angles of one triangle equal two angles of another triangle, the triangles are similar. Two pairs of equal angles are sufficient - no third angle is needed.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Confirm the third angles are implicitly equal anyway. Angle sum: $\angle F = 180^\circ - 40^\circ - 95^\circ = 45^\circ$ and $\angle T = 180^\circ - 40^\circ - 95^\circ = 45^\circ$. They are forced equal by the first two pairs.
